stories.headline: Zelenskyy urges peace talks as Russia rejects ceasefire offer amid ongoing Ukraine war

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: Henry Nowak died the same way a civilization dies: abandoned, handcuffed by authorities who neither trusted nor cared for him, and accused of hate crimes he did not commit.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Unfortunately, the Russian side once again chooses war – everyone heard the response today. Weak response. He simply does not want to end the war.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: We brought the war onto your territory, and you would not have been able to cope with it without North Korea’s help.

stories.headline: India secures massive $30B data center investment boosting global AI and cloud infrastructure

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: India’s digital infrastructure journey is gathering remarkable momentum. AirTrunk has announced plans to invest around Rs. 3 lakh crore ($30 billion) in India, and develop 5 GW of data centre capacity.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Japan is converting used cooking oil into sustainable aviation fuel to power greener flights. Cuba battles blackouts, shortages and a worsening economic crisis. India's economy grew 7.7%, but inflation and oil prices remain major concerns

stories.headline: Senate Republicans greenlight dropping IRS investigations into Trump

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: At 4am, Senate Republicans gave the greenlight for the IRS to drop ALL investigations into Trump and his family. That means if Trump is evading taxes, we’ll never know. I have a bill to make this illegal. And I won’t stop fighting to get it done.

stories.headline: Senate advances SAVE America Act on election integrity with partisan votes

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: I’m about to offer the House-passed version of the SAVE America Act on the Senate floor in connection with tonight’s vote-a-rama This one should get at least 51 votes Will keep you posted

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Florida processes more than 10 million votes in a matter of hours. California takes days — or sometimes even weeks — to count the votes. It’s pathetic — and it’s corrosive to our civic culture.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: I asked Senate Republicans who among them thought Trump’s slush fund was a good idea. None replied and silence filled the chamber. I then offered Republicans a chance to shut it down by voting for my amendment. They chose not to. They are simply too scared of crossing this corrupt president.

stories.headline: White House announces strong US jobs numbers

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: More than half of the publicly identified donors to President Trump’s White House ballroom project have won new or expanded federal contracts worth more than $50 billion, according to a report from a government watchdog group.

stories.headline: S&P 500 erases nearly $2 trillion after strong jobs report, Bitcoin down over 50% from highs
stories.body:
What just happened?

The S&P 500 just erased nearly -$2 TRILLION of market cap just hours after 3rd strongest US jobs report in 18 months.

Meanwhile, Bitcoin is officially down over -50% from its record high in October 2025.

What's happening? Let us explain.

(a thread)

stories.headline: Tesla invites to CVPR booth for neural video generation, Optimus, and real FSD demos.
stories.body:
Come visit our CVPR booth for hands-on experiences:

– Neural video generation + sim drive
– Selfies with @Tesla_Optimus 
– Real FSD test drives in Tesla models of your choice

Chat directly with the team behind it, including @aelluswamy & @pduan and other engineers to get answers to your questions. Recruiters are also on-site for career opportunities.

While others talk about autonomous driving, Tesla is the only one solving it in the real world with a true long-term approach. Come experience live demos and grab some swag!

stories.headline: MIT researcher unveils self-evolving AI scientist framework for principled discovery.
stories.body:
We've made a breakthrough in self-evolving AI scientists moving from "search" to "principled discovery": Scientific discovery requires that the search space itself changes, and an AI scientist must perceive this shift without intervention. We built an AI that achieves this for the first time with the ability to discover the scientific vocabulary it reasons in. Evidence, tools, artifacts, verifiers, failures & claims become typed provenance. We show three distinct modalities: 1) retrieval, adding known objects; 2) search, exploring a fixed schema; and critically: 3) discovery, a verified regime transition. 

We solve the open-endedness evaluation problem by lifting agentic workflows into a typed copresheaf and proving, via a Kan obstruction, that true discovery is not unbounded generation but a verifiable schema expansion: old evidence is transported by Left Kan extension, and genuine novelty is mathematically quantified by the pointwise residual beyond the transported image - separating discovery from mere search and making novelty objective and measurable rather than a subjective judgment or benchmark delta.

Our AI scientist is built in a way that does not pre-conceive the approach it chooses; instead, we endow the system with formal power to adapt, evolve, and reason from first principles. Case studies include: 
1⃣Builder/Breaker model that discovers mode-conditioned compliance in proteins;
2⃣CategoryScienceClaw that finds anisotropic fiber-network stiffness rules.

Great work in collaboration with my graduate student @fwang108_ @MITdeptofBE

F.Y. Wang & M.J. Buehler, Self-Revising Discovery Systems for Science: A Categorical Framework for Agentic Artificial Intelligence, arXiv:2606.01444, 2026

stories.headline: Special ops training with aircraft in Long Beach and Pasadena areas of Southern California.
stories.body:
Special operations forces conducted realistic training in an urban environment overnight in the Long Beach/Pasadena areas of Southern California, including aircraft of the U.S. Army's 160th Special Operations Aviation Regiment (SOAR). This training is conducted by U.S. Army Delta Force, U.S. Navy SEALs, and other special forces units in major cities around the country several times per year.

Video from Long Beach Local News

stories.headline: Update on rescued sea lion pup Hollander from Newport Beach
stories.body:
Pacific Marina Mammal Center in California posted an update about the rescued sea lion named Hollander via Instagram 🥺

“Remember Hollander? She is the California sea lion pup who stranded on March 5th in Newport Beach, a day when Ian Slater had been invited to come for a visit to see our hospital in action. 

Hollander was named after the famous Heated Rivalry star [Shane Hollander] and she has been a star of a patient, recovering from malnutrition, external injuries to her flippers, and an ingestion of a fishing hook, with the dedicated help from our expert team of staff and volunteers 🩺

Hollander has eaten her fair share of fish, successfully treated for injuries, gained weight, and ready to go home with her fellow rehabilitated friend, Lighthouse 🩵”

🔗
